fixtureDescription: Middle school passage — plastic pollution and student activism
passage:
These days, many young people are becoming more aware of environmental problems. Plastic pollution, in particular, has become a serious issue that we cannot ignore. Every year, millions of tons of plastic waste end up in the ocean, where they harm marine life and damage ecosystems.

Sarah, a fifteen-year-old student from Busan, decided to take action after watching a documentary about sea turtles. "I was shocked when I saw a turtle struggling because of a plastic straw," she said. "I wanted to make a difference, even if it was small." She started a club at her school that encourages students to bring reusable water bottles instead of disposable ones. The school now runs the program in every classroom.

The club has grown rapidly. As a result, the school has reduced its plastic waste by nearly forty percent in just six months. Sarah's teacher, Ms. Kim, points out that small changes can lead to big results. "When students learn to face environmental challenges and address them directly, they carry that mindset into adulthood," she explained.

Of course, individual efforts alone cannot solve the problem. Governments and companies must also play a role. However, students like Sarah show us that change often begins with one person who refuses to look away.
